match.userMessage:
9-panel cinematic storyboard sheet derived from Image 1. Mix Wide, Medium, and Tight shots of the same scene. No borders between panels.

Layout: 3x3 grid (9 panels) in a 16:9 landscape image.

Scene: Modern social-first product photography with natural cinematic beach lifestyle energy, SCARLETT wearing light casual beach clothing suitable for walking and carrying a canvas tote bag, her hand tensed and reaching across to lift the small coral box from the pale marble surface of the white vanity about to flip open the lid, inside a bright Bondi studio apartment featuring an open window with ocean view, detailed environment of crisp white walls and fresh coastal morning ambiance with bright whites and pale marble tones, bright natural window sunlight slicing across the surface creating clean directional shadows and immediate real light feel, eye-level slightly above angle with shallow depth of field on the tactile interaction, dynamic handheld composition capturing potential energy in the mid-reach gesture, fresh sensory effortlessly cool mood with high-key airy atmosphere and pops of contrast, crisp whites and warm natural tones, clean bright true-to-life color grading.

Match Image 1 exactly: same character(s) (face, hair, skin, clothing), lighting, color grade, and texture. If character reference sheets are provided, use them for likeness — do not render them as separate panels.

No text, dialogue bubbles, scene numbers, or watermarks.


  <reference-images>
    - Image 1 [PRIMARY SOURCE]: Primary source scene — generate 9 variant shots from this image
    - Image 2 [CHARACTER REF]: Scarlett - Match the real-world appearance of Sienna Blake exactly
    - Image 3 [LOCATION REF]: INT. BONDI STUDIO - DAY
    
    IMPORTANT: Character, location, and element reference images are for IDENTITY CONSISTENCY ONLY. Do NOT reproduce them as separate panels or subjects. Elements (logos, products) must render faithfully when referenced but should appear naturally within the scene. All output panels must depict the scene from the PRIMARY SOURCE image.
  </reference-images>
